name: Catalan Bouillabaisse
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the flesh of the red snapper into 2-inch thick strips. Set aside.
2. Add the bones and trimmings from the fish to the boiling salted water. Cook until reduced by 50%. Strain the fish stock. Set aside.
3. Heat the butter and oil in a heavy saucepan or Dutch oven. Add the onions. Cook until transparent.
4. Add the tomatoes. Simmer until very tender (about 5 minutes).
5. Add the reserved fish stock, parsley, bay leaf, and thyme to the saucepan. Bring to a boil.
6. Add the wine, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 15 minutes.
7. Preheat the oven to 400 degrees F.
8. Finely chop the almonds and garlic using a grinder, mortar and pestle, or blender to make them into a paste.
9. Add the paste to the simmering wine sauce. Stir until thoroughly blended.
10. Place the red snapper slices in the bottom of a well-buttered (2 1/2-3 quart) casserole. Pour the sauce over. Cover.
11. Bake until the broth begins to simmer. Bake another 20 minutes. Serve from the casserole.
